name: python3-pastescript
title: python3-pastescript
summary: PasteScript is a pluggable command-line tool (Python3)
description:
PasteScript is a pluggable command-line tool (Python3)
Paster is pluggable command-line frontend, including commands to setup
package file layouts. By this you are able to create file layouts for
packages and serve webapps quickly.
.
Built-in features:
* Creating file layouts for packages.
For instance a setuptools-ready file layout.
* Serving up web applications, with configuration based on
https://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/pastedeploy/en/latest/
(available within Debian based distribution as python3-pastedeploy)
.
This package provides the Python 3.x module.

commands.name: paster3
commands.description: pluggable command-line frontend, including commands to setup package file layouts

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use paster3 because PasteScript is a pluggable command-line tool (Python3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use paster3 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `paster3 paster3 [OPTION] COMMAND [command_options]` as documented upstream for paster3.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair paster3 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `paster3 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
